rationale: The citing paper uses Amir and Levav (2008) to support a key mechanism in its theory: adding a third option can let decision makers avoid detailed attribute trade-offs and rely on contextual cues (intermediate/dominating options). This citation is tied to interpretation of Studies 3-5, where the authors test and moderate that process account. It is more than a generic literature-list mention, even though it is not the sole basis of the model.
relation_description: The 2018 paper draws on Amir and Levav (2008) as prior conceptual evidence that trinary choice contexts can shift decision strategy away from trade-off-based preference evaluation. That prior insight is used to justify and interpret the paper's hierarchical decision-process explanation of context effects.
